metadata.description: Evaluation dataset for RAG system using Open Education Handbook

questions.question: What does OER stand for?
questions.reference_answer: Open Educational Resources

questions.question: What are the 5 Rs of OER?
questions.reference_answer: The 5 Rs are: Retain, Reuse, Revise, Remix, and Redistribute

questions.question: What is Creative Commons licensing?
questions.reference_answer: Creative Commons is a licensing system that allows creators to specify how others can use their work, providing a standardized way to grant copyright permissions

questions.question: What are the main benefits of using Open Educational Resources?
questions.reference_answer: Benefits include cost savings for students, flexibility to adapt materials, improved access to education, ability to update content quickly, and collaborative improvement of resources

questions.question: How can educators evaluate the quality of OER materials?
questions.reference_answer: Quality can be evaluated by assessing accuracy, relevance, clarity, technical quality, appropriateness for audience, alignment with learning objectives, and pedagogical effectiveness

questions.question: What is the difference between open access and open education?
questions.reference_answer: Open access refers to free availability of research publications, while open education encompasses broader educational practices including OER, open pedagogy, and open educational practices

questions.question: What are some common barriers to OER adoption?
questions.reference_answer: Common barriers include lack of awareness, concerns about quality, difficulty finding relevant resources, lack of technical skills, institutional resistance, and sustainability challenges

questions.question: What is open pedagogy?
questions.reference_answer: Open pedagogy is a teaching approach that leverages open educational resources and practices to create collaborative, student-centered learning experiences where learners contribute to knowledge creation

questions.question: How do OER repositories help educators?
questions.reference_answer: OER repositories provide centralized access to educational resources, offer search and discovery tools, ensure quality through curation, and facilitate sharing and collaboration among educators

questions.question: What role does metadata play in OER discoverability?
questions.reference_answer: Metadata helps make OER discoverable by providing standardized descriptions of resources including subject, education level, format, language, and licensing information

questions.question: What are MOOCs?
questions.reference_answer: MOOCs are Massive Open Online Courses - online courses designed for unlimited participation and open access via the internet

questions.question: How can institutions support OER creation and adoption?
questions.reference_answer: Institutions can provide funding, training, technical infrastructure, recognition and incentives for faculty, policy support, and dedicated staff for OER initiatives

questions.question: What is the UNESCO declaration on OER?
questions.reference_answer: The UNESCO OER declaration is a policy framework that encourages governments to support the creation, use, and sharing of open educational resources to expand access to quality education

questions.question: What technical skills do educators need to create OER?
questions.reference_answer: Educators need skills in content authoring tools, understanding of open licenses, basic multimedia editing, file format selection, and online platforms for sharing resources

questions.question: How does OER relate to copyright?
questions.reference_answer: OER uses open licenses like Creative Commons that work within copyright law to give creators control while allowing others to use, adapt, and share materials more freely than traditional copyright

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.reference_answer: Paris

questions.question: How does quantum computing work?
questions.reference_answer: Quantum computing uses quantum mechanical phenomena like superposition and entanglement to perform computations

questions.question: Who won the 2024 World Cup?
questions.reference_answer: This information would not be in the 2017 Open Education Handbook

questions.question: What is the speed of light?
questions.reference_answer: Approximately 299,792,458 meters per second

questions.question: How do neural networks learn?
questions.reference_answer: Neural networks learn by adjusting weights through backpropagation based on the difference between predicted and actual outputs
